problem: The owner cannot tell during the week whether higher sales are actually producing higher operating profit.
wedge: A read-only weekly profit health check generated from uploaded files, with no live banking connection and no accounting write-back.
primaryJob: Understand estimated operating profit for the week and resolve the few items preventing verification.
primaryArtifact: A source-linked Verified Weekly Salon Brief.

rejectedAlternatives.alternative: Full bookkeeping sync with write-back to QuickBooks or Xero.
rejectedAlternatives.reason: Requires write authority and ongoing reconciliation, which explodes scope far past a one-week read-only health check.

rejectedAlternatives.alternative: Live bank API connection via Plaid.
rejectedAlternatives.reason: Adds credential handling and compliance surface; this slice must stay file-upload only so no live banking is touched.
Regulated advice outside the profit-visibility job and explicitly prohibited for this wedge.
The uncertainty is per-salon per-week; a multi-location roll-up dilutes the wedge and the buyer.

openUnknowns: Whether owners will upload all four file types every week or only a subset.
How many transactions typically lack a matching receipt and therefore block verification.
Whether square_export payout timing aligns with the bank_csv week boundary.
successCondition: The owner can read an estimated weekly operating profit for the salon and resolve every verification item blocking that estimate, using only the uploaded files.

propose: flag transactions that need owner classification
request a missing receipt for an unmatched expense
